instruction: Write a SysML v2 model with a part that performs a two-step inspection action.
output: package SeedAction001 { part def Inspector { perform action inspect { action start; action finish; } } }

instruction: Write a SysML v2 model with a part that performs a three-step workflow action.
output: package SeedAction002 { part def Workflow { perform action inspect { action a; action b; action c; } } }

instruction: Write a SysML v2 model with a part that owns an integer attribute.
output: package SeedAttribute001 { part def Camera { attribute resolution : ScalarValues::Integer; } part camera : Camera; }

instruction: Write a SysML v2 model with two parts and a connection between their ports.
output: package SeedConnection001 { part def A { port p; } part def B { port q; } part def Top { part a : A; part b : B; connect a.p to b.q; } }

instruction: Write a SysML v2 model containing a measured part and a positive mass constraint.
output: package SeedConstraint001 { private import SI::*; part def Vehicle { attribute mass : MassValue = 1200 [kg]; constraint positiveMass { mass > 0 [kg] } } }

instruction: Write a SysML v2 model with item definitions and item usages.
output: package SeedItem001 { item def Signal; part def Controller { item input : Signal; item output : Signal; } }

instruction: Write a nested SysML v2 system model with subsystem and component definitions.
output: package SeedNested001 { part def System { part def Subsystem { part def Component { attribute id : ScalarValues::Integer; } part component : Component; } part subsystem : Subsystem; } part system : System; }

instruction: Write a SysML v2 fleet hierarchy with repeated vehicle usages.
output: package SeedNested002 { part def Fleet { part def Vehicle { part def Engine { attribute rpm : ScalarValues::Real; } part engine : Engine; } part lead : Vehicle; part spare : Vehicle; } part fleet : Fleet; }

instruction: Write a SysML v2 model with a vehicle part definition and a typed engine part.
output: package SeedPart001 { part def Engine; part def Vehicle { part engine : Engine; } }

instruction: Write a SysML v2 model with ports on two part definitions.
output: package SeedPort001 { part def Producer { port output; } part def Consumer { port input; } }

instruction: Write a nested SysML v2 model with a controller part and ports.
output: package SeedPort002 { part def Controller { port command; port telemetry; attribute health : ScalarValues::Real; } part controller : Controller; }

instruction: Write a SysML v2 model containing a requirement definition and a system part.
output: package SeedRequirement001 { requirement def MassLimit; part def Vehicle { attribute mass : ScalarValues::Real; } }

instruction: Write a SysML v2 model with one part definition specializing another.
output: package SeedSpecialization001 { part def BaseModule; part def PayloadModule :> BaseModule; }

instruction: Write a SysML v2 model with a three-state lifecycle.
output: package SeedState002 { part def Machine { state def Lifecycle { state idle; state armed; state firing; transition first idle then armed; transition first armed then firing; } } }

instruction: Write a SysML v2 model with a base part definition and a specialized subsystem.
output: package SeedSubsetting001 { part def Module; part def Subsystem :> Module { attribute load : ScalarValues::Real; } part subsystem : Subsystem; }
